ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 1 - Introduction to Data Analytics and the Analytics Lifecycle
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to distinguish among descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics by explaining the unique question each type answers and its position on the analytics spectrum.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 1 - Introduction to Data Analytics and the Analytics Lifecycle
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to sequence the six phases of the CRISP-DM framework in the correct order and describe the primary objective of each phase.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 1 - Introduction to Data Analytics and the Analytics Lifecycle
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to classify a given real-world business scenario to the appropriate type of analytics based on the nature of the question being answered.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 1 - Introduction to Data Analytics and the Analytics Lifecycle
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to map at least one foundational case study to the corresponding phases of the CRISP-DM lifecycle, identifying how each phase is reflected in the case's analytical approach.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 1 - Introduction to Data Analytics and the Analytics Lifecycle
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to justify how data analytics creates competitive advantage for organizations by connecting specific industry applications to measurable business outcomes.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 2 - Ethics, Data Types, and Data Sources in Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to distinguish between structured and unstructured data types based on their organizational characteristics, storage requirements, and suitability for different analytical tasks.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 2 - Ethics, Data Types, and Data Sources in Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to evaluate a given data source for quality, reliability, and appropriateness using established criteria prior to incorporating it into an analytics project.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 2 - Ethics, Data Types, and Data Sources in Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to identify specific points in the analytics pipeline where bias can be introduced through data collection, model design, or interpretation.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 2 - Ethics, Data Types, and Data Sources in Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to apply core ethical principles — including fairness, transparency, accountability, and data minimization — to a described data analytics scenario to determine whether the practices are ethically sound.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 2 - Ethics, Data Types, and Data Sources in Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to compare data collection methods — including web APIs, web scraping, and database querying — according to their trade-offs in automation, data quality, access complexity, and ethical considerations.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 3 - Data Quality Assessment and Data Cleaning Fundamentals
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to classify a dataset's quality issues according to the dimensions of accuracy, completeness, consistency, and reliability.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 3 - Data Quality Assessment and Data Cleaning Fundamentals
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to distinguish between the three mechanisms of missingness — MCAR, MAR, and MNAR — based on patterns observed in a dataset.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 3 - Data Quality Assessment and Data Cleaning Fundamentals
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to apply statistical and visual techniques to detect outliers, duplicate records, and data inconsistencies in a raw dataset.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 3 - Data Quality Assessment and Data Cleaning Fundamentals
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct a systematic, repeatable data cleaning workflow that addresses missing values, duplicates, outliers, and formatting inconsistencies in sequence.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 3 - Data Quality Assessment and Data Cleaning Fundamentals
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to evaluate whether a cleaned dataset meets predefined quality standards by executing final validation checks prior to downstream analysis.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 4 - Database Basics and Relational Data Concepts
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to distinguish between databases, spreadsheets, and flat files by comparing their structural characteristics and appropriate use cases.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 4 - Database Basics and Relational Data Concepts
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to identify primary keys, foreign keys, and composite keys within a given relational database table structure.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 4 - Database Basics and Relational Data Concepts
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to classify the relationship type between two tables — one-to-one, one-to-many, or many-to-many — when presented with a schema diagram.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 4 - Database Basics and Relational Data Concepts
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to interpret a database schema diagram by identifying its tables, columns, data types, and inter-table relationships.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: 4 - Database Basics and Relational Data Concepts
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to explain how foreign key relationships between tables determine the structure of multi-table SQL JOIN queries.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Excel Functions and Statistical Tools
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct INDEX-MATCH formulas to retrieve data from multi-table datasets where VLOOKUP's structural limitations would produce errors or incorrect results.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Excel Functions and Statistical Tools
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to build conditional aggregation formulas using SUMIF, COUNTIF, or AVERAGEIF to compute targeted summary metrics for specified data subsets.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Excel Functions and Statistical Tools
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to calculate descriptive statistics — including mean, median, standard deviation, and range — for a dataset using Excel's built-in statistical functions.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Excel Functions and Statistical Tools
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to design a PivotTable that groups and aggregates raw data into a structured summary suitable for business reporting.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Excel Functions and Statistical Tools
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to interpret descriptive statistics and conditional aggregation outputs from a real-world dataset to derive evidence-based analytical insights.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Pivot Tables, Dashboards, and Power Query in Excel
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct a pivot table in Excel by configuring rows, columns, values, and summary functions to summarize a structured dataset.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Pivot Tables, Dashboards, and Power Query in Excel
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to apply advanced pivot table features — including calculated fields, slicers, and timeline filters — to segment and explore data at multiple levels of granularity.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Pivot Tables, Dashboards, and Power Query in Excel
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to design an interactive Excel dashboard by assembling pivot charts, slicers, timeline controls, and KPI summary cells into a cohesive, usable layout.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Pivot Tables, Dashboards, and Power Query in Excel
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to transform raw data using Power Query by removing errors, reshaping columns, assigning correct data types, and adding custom calculated columns.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Pivot Tables, Dashboards, and Power Query in Excel
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to configure Power Query connection properties and refresh workflows so that linked pivot tables and dashboards automatically reflect updated source data.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Visualization Principles and Excel Charting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to select the appropriate chart type for a given dataset by applying the comparison, composition, distribution, and relationship decision framework.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Visualization Principles and Excel Charting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct a formatted chart in Excel that incorporates visual hierarchy through purposeful use of color, size, contrast, and layout.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Visualization Principles and Excel Charting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to detect misleading visual representations in charts by identifying specific design flaws such as truncated axes, distorted proportions, and inappropriate scaling.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Visualization Principles and Excel Charting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to evaluate a completed Excel visual summary against established design principles to determine whether it accurately and clearly communicates the intended data story.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Visualization Principles and Excel Charting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to justify chart design decisions by referencing the ethical responsibilities associated with honest data representation.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Fundamentals: Queries, Filtering, and Sorting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to identify the roles of tables, rows, columns, and primary keys in the structure of a relational database.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Fundamentals: Queries, Filtering, and Sorting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct syntactically correct SELECT queries that retrieve specific columns from a database table.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Fundamentals: Queries, Filtering, and Sorting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to apply WHERE clause conditions — including comparison operators, logical operators (AND, OR, NOT), and NULL handling — to filter rows from a dataset.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Fundamentals: Queries, Filtering, and Sorting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to sequence query results using ORDER BY across single and multiple columns in ascending or descending order.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Fundamentals: Queries, Filtering, and Sorting
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to compose multi-clause SQL queries combining SELECT, WHERE, ORDER BY, and LIMIT to answer targeted business questions using real datasets.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Joins, Relationships, and Advanced Query Techniques
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to distinguish between INNER, LEFT OUTER, RIGHT OUTER, and FULL OUTER JOIN operations by identifying which records each type includes or excludes from a multi-table result set.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Joins, Relationships, and Advanced Query Techniques
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct multi-table SQL queries that use the appropriate JOIN type, table aliases, and ON conditions to retrieve accurate relational data from two or more linked tables.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Joins, Relationships, and Advanced Query Techniques
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to write GROUP BY queries that apply aggregate functions and filter grouped results using the HAVING clause to produce per-group summaries for analytical reporting.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Joins, Relationships, and Advanced Query Techniques
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to implement subqueries in WHERE and FROM clauses — including correlated and non-correlated patterns — to stage intermediate results within a larger SQL statement.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: SQL Joins, Relationships, and Advanced Query Techniques
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to evaluate a complex multi-table analytical problem and select an appropriate combination of JOIN operations, subqueries, and aggregation techniques by applying a structured query design decision-making framework.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Python Basics for Data Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to configure a Python analytics environment, including installing required libraries and verifying the setup with a test script.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Python Basics for Data Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to select the appropriate Python data structure — list, tuple, or dictionary — for a given analytics task based on the nature of the data and required operations.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Python Basics for Data Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct control structures, including conditionals and loops, to filter and process records in a dataset.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Python Basics for Data Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to define modular Python functions that encapsulate reusable data processing or summary calculation logic.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Python Basics for Data Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to write a complete end-to-end Python script that reads a CSV dataset, applies control structures and custom functions to process the data, and outputs summary results.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Manipulation and Cleaning with NumPy and Pandas
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct NumPy arrays and apply vectorized operations and universal functions to perform efficient numerical computations on datasets.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Manipulation and Cleaning with NumPy and Pandas
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to select specific subsets of a Pandas DataFrame using label-based, position-based, and conditional filtering techniques.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Manipulation and Cleaning with NumPy and Pandas
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to integrate multiple datasets into a unified structure by applying concatenation, merge, and join operations with appropriate join types.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Manipulation and Cleaning with NumPy and Pandas
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to evaluate missing data patterns in a dataset and justify the selection of an appropriate handling strategy — removal, imputation, or interpolation — based on the type of missingness present.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Data Manipulation and Cleaning with NumPy and Pandas
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to execute an end-to-end data cleaning workflow on a real-world dataset, correcting data types, removing duplicates and outliers, standardizing string columns, and exporting a validated, analysis-ready output.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Descriptive Statistics and Probability Distributions
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to calculate measures of central tendency, spread, and skewness for a dataset using Python libraries such as NumPy and SciPy.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Descriptive Statistics and Probability Distributions
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to interpret the relationship between mean, median, and skewness to characterize the shape and symmetry of a data distribution.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Descriptive Statistics and Probability Distributions
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to differentiate between the normal, binomial, and Poisson distributions by identifying the conditions and parameter types that define each.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Descriptive Statistics and Probability Distributions
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to compute probabilities for the normal, binomial, and Poisson distributions using Python's scipy.stats library.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Descriptive Statistics and Probability Distributions
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct visualizations of probability distributions in Python to communicate how changes in distribution parameters affect shape and spread.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Hypothesis Testing, Correlation, and Regression Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct null and alternative hypotheses for a given research question and identify the appropriate statistical test based on the data type and research context.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Hypothesis Testing, Correlation, and Regression Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to interpret p-values and effect sizes within the context of a hypothesis test, distinguishing between statistical significance and practical significance.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Hypothesis Testing, Correlation, and Regression Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to select between Pearson and Spearman correlation coefficients based on the distributional properties of the variables and justify the choice.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Hypothesis Testing, Correlation, and Regression Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to build a multiple linear regression model from a real dataset, interpret each estimated coefficient, and assess model fit using R² and tests of statistical significance.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Hypothesis Testing, Correlation, and Regression Analysis
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to translate regression and correlation results into plain-language insights that address a defined business or research question for a non-technical audience.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Data Visualization with Python Libraries
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to distinguish between the roles of Matplotlib and Seaborn in the Python visualization ecosystem, including how Seaborn extends Matplotlib's rendering capabilities.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Data Visualization with Python Libraries
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to select the appropriate chart type — bar chart, line plot, scatter plot, histogram, heatmap, or pair plot — based on the nature of the data and the analytical question being addressed.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Data Visualization with Python Libraries
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to construct publication-quality figures in Python by applying themes, color palettes, custom typography, annotations, and layout adjustments using Matplotlib and Seaborn.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Data Visualization with Python Libraries
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to evaluate a data visualization for misleading design choices — such as truncated axes, inconsistent scales, or inappropriate chart selection — that distort the story the data communicates.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Advanced Data Visualization with Python Libraries
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to build an interactive figure using Plotly that incorporates hover tooltips or click events to enable dynamic data exploration within a Python-based workflow.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Introduction to Machine Learning for Business Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to distinguish between supervised and unsupervised learning by classifying a given business problem into the appropriate machine learning paradigm based on data availability and desired output.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Introduction to Machine Learning for Business Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to select an appropriate machine learning algorithm — linear regression, decision trees, or k-means clustering — for a described business scenario by matching algorithm characteristics to problem type and data structure.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Introduction to Machine Learning for Business Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to interpret model evaluation metrics — including accuracy, precision, recall, and error measures — in terms of their business implications for a given analytics use case.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Introduction to Machine Learning for Business Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to execute an end-to-end machine learning workflow — from problem definition and data preparation through model training, evaluation, and insight communication — applied to a realistic business analytics scenario.

ModuleOutcomes.ModuleDisplayName: Introduction to Machine Learning for Business Analytics
ModuleOutcomes.Text: By the end of this module, students will be able to identify real-world business applications of machine learning, such as customer segmentation, sales forecasting, and fraud detection, by mapping each application to its corresponding algorithm and learning paradigm.
